description:
You are assisting a capacity–planning engineer who keeps a **text-based snapshot** of the
firewall rules (one rule per line, no comments) in

/home/user/firewall/current_rules.list

Before you start, the file already exists and contains exactly the three
lines shown below _in this order_:

-A INPUT -p tcp --dport 22 -j ACCEPT  
-A INPUT -p icmp -j ACCEPT  
-A INPUT -m conntrack --ctstate ESTABLISHED,RELATED -j ACCEPT

The engineer needs to open TCP port 9090 for an internal monitoring
dashboard and maintain a reproducible audit trail.

Your task (all work must be done from the command line, without elevated
privileges):

1. Create a **new** file named  
   /home/user/firewall/updated_rules.list  
   that is an exact copy of current_rules.list but with one additional
   rule appended as its very last line:

   -A INPUT -p tcp --dport 9090 -j ACCEPT

   • Do **not** modify current_rules.list itself.  
   • The resulting updated_rules.list must therefore contain **four**
     lines in total.

2. Produce a two-line audit report called  
   /home/user/firewall/update_report.log  
   with the **exact** format shown below (no extra spaces or blank lines):

   NEW_RULE_ADDED: OK  
   INPUT_RULE_COUNT: 4

   • “4” must reflect the total number of lines (rules) now present in
     updated_rules.list.  
   • The test harness is case- and punctuation-sensitive, so be sure to
     match the sample exactly.

A correct solution will be verified this way:

• The file /home/user/firewall/updated_rules.list exists.  
• Its four lines, in order, are the three originals followed by the new
  port-9090 rule.  
• /home/user/firewall/update_report.log exists and contains exactly the
  two lines specified above.

You only need 2–3 typical shell commands (e.g. cp, echo, wc, etc.) to
finish the task. The agent is free to display any console output, but the
two files must exactly match the specifications for automated grading.
truth:
Initial state (pre-installed for the grader):
Directory: /home/user/firewall/

File: /home/user/firewall/current_rules.list  
Contents (including final newline):

-A INPUT -p tcp --dport 22 -j ACCEPT
-A INPUT -p icmp -j ACCEPT
-A INPUT -m conntrack --ctstate ESTABLISHED,RELATED -j ACCEPT

Expected files after successful agent run:

1. /home/user/firewall/updated_rules.list  
   Expected contents (including final newline):

-A INPUT -p tcp --dport 22 -j ACCEPT
-A INPUT -p icmp -j ACCEPT
-A INPUT -m conntrack --ctstate ESTABLISHED,RELATED -j ACCEPT
-A INPUT -p tcp --dport 9090 -j ACCEPT

2. /home/user/firewall/update_report.log  
   Expected contents (including final newline):

NEW_RULE_ADDED: OK
INPUT_RULE_COUNT: 4
name: task_000000_6d56eecc
